title: Factsheet: A joint commitment for culture in the European Union

title: Europe for Culture – Culture for Europe: a long-term strategic vision for culture in Europe
content:
Europe's three main EU institutions have united behind an unprecedented political commitment to place culture at the centre of the union's strategic agenda.

The European Parliament, the Council of the European Union and the European Commission have signed a Joint Declaration setting out twelve guiding principles for cultural policy across Europe. The declaration positions culture as both a strategic resource for, and a beneficiary of, European unity, democracy and competitiveness.

In an era of rapid change and new challenges for the world, the Joint Declaration places culture at the heart of Europe’s identity by reaffirming its role as a fundamental pillar of European values, resilience and sustainable growth. It recognises culture and cultural heritage as a public good, while acknowledging culture’s intrinsic, societal, civic and economic value, and the EU’s role as a global cultural powerhouse.

Inspired by the Culture Compass for Europe, the Joint Declaration sets out twelve guiding principles for cultural policies in Europe on:

  • Freedom of artistic expression and the right to create
  • Cultural and linguistic diversity
  • Access to culture for all
snippet: Europe's three main EU institutions have united behind an unprecedented political commitment to place culture at the centre of the union's strategic agenda.
creator: holbobe

title: Get ready for the 2026 European Authors Day
content:
The organisers of the European Authors Day invite cultural institutions, businesses, event organisers, educators, local authorities and all interested stakeholders to join the online webinar to learn more about the 2026 edition of the event series.

The hosts of the webinar will discuss:

  • available opportunities in 2026
  • what the 2026 welcome packs will include
  • tips and tricks on how to organise great events

    • Tuesday 16 June 2026 11:30 (CET)

    Join the webinar to learn about the 2026 edition of the European Authors Day.

    Page update:

    Please note this event is now over. Thank you to everyone who attended.
